message:
fix(actor): assert mask_depth stays within MAX_MASK_DEPTH in on_stop

Add a defensive assertion around the mask_depth increment in the
actor on_stop path. Previously, run_actor_loop incremented
inner.mask_depth unconditionally before invoking actor.on_stop(),
which could silently violate INV-MASK-BOUNDED if the depth saturated
or wrapped — causing cancellation signals to never be observed by
the actor, making bugs extremely hard to diagnose.

The new code takes the write lock, asserts
guard.mask_depth < crate::types::task_context::MAX_MASK_DEPTH with
a detailed panic message naming the invariant, and only then performs
the increment. The explicit scope block also ensures the write guard
is released before the async on_stop call (which previously held it
only until the drop at the end of the single statement).

This is a pure correctness guard; no behavior changes in the happy
path where mask_depth is well within MAX_MASK_DEPTH.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
